overview.definition: TB is a lung infection caused by bacteria called Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Think of it as germs attacking your lungs.
overview.severity: TB is serious but completely curable with proper treatment
overview.contagious: Yes - spreads through air when someone with TB coughs or sneezes

common: Bad cough lasting more than 3 weeks
Chest pain when breathing or coughing
symptoms.timeline: Symptoms develop slowly over weeks or months
when_to_see_doctor: Night sweats for more than 2 weeks
Contact with someone who has TB

transmission.how_it_spreads: TB spreads through the air when someone coughs or sneezes. It's like catching a cold, but more serious.
risk_factors: People living in high TB areas

latent_vs_active.latent: You have TB bacteria but feel fine and can't spread it
latent_vs_active.active: You feel sick and can spread TB to others

tests: Chest X-ray - Shows spots or holes in lungs
Sputum test - Checks mucus for TB bacteria
xray_findings: White spots in upper parts of lungs

treatment.overview: TB can be 100% cured with medicine
important_notes: Don't stop early even if you feel better
After 2 weeks of treatment, you're much less contagious
Stopping early makes bacteria resistant to medicine

tips: Wear mask if you have active TB
Keep windows open for fresh air
Get tested if exposed to TB

untreated: TB can spread to other parts of body
Can be fatal if not treated

recovery_timeline: Week 1-2: Start feeling a bit better
Month 6: Fully cured (if completed all medicine)
